multiplechoiceresponse.label: You train an optimal classification tree to audit which labels were flipped in a fairness-aware model. The tree splits on “race = minority” and “prior convictions > 2”. What does this indicate?

choicegroup.choice: Flips are concentrated among minority individuals with multiple prior convictions.
The tree shows spurious splits that have no relation to flips.
Flips occurred equally across all groups but the tree happened to pick these features.
The model is highly complex and overfitting to noise in the data.

div.p: As an interpretability tool, classification trees are trained to predict which labels were flipped; its first splits reveal the strongest patterns in those flipping decisions. If the tree’s primary splits involve race and prior convictions, this indicates that flips are systematically associated with those characteristics. The tree is not being trained to predict outcomes, but to explain the optimization’s flipping decisions. Therefore, these splits reveal patterns in where fairness interventions were applied.
